channel.title: CrossWire
channel.description: Curated briefings from MOCR — Hermes, Tech, and Commanders

item.title: CrossWire — Evening Brief, July 24
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — July 24 Evening

🚀 Release Radar: v0.19.0 The Quicksilver Release (July 20)

Still the big news from the weekend: Hermes Agent v0.19.0 — ~2,245 commits, ~1,065 merged PRs, ~3,300 issues closed, 450+ community contributors. First-turn time-to-first-token down ~80% across every surface, reasoning streams live by default, desktop got a 20-PR speed overhaul (14× faster streaming markdown), smart approvals, Bitwarden/1Password auth, durable delivery ledger, and subscription management from the terminal. We're running on it right now.

🔍 Trending PRs — July 24

  • #70875 — Windows text-mode subprocess decode bug, final close (teknium1) — The megasweep: all 163 remaining text=True subprocess call sites now pass encoding="utf-8", errors="replace", plus a new CI linter fails any future text=True without explicit encoding. Labels touch everything — agent, CLI, gateway, cron, plugins, tools, LSP, every platform. The 21-location bug class from #53428 is finally dead.
  • #66510 — Bound multimodal outer-loop retries (stigrunar) — Vision worker content as typed parts could crash re.sub (expected string, got list), causing infinite retry loops. P3, needs-decision.
  • #70925 — Desktop terminal font picker (Zeraphim) — Settings → Appearance now has a profile-scoped font selector. Pick a Nerd Font like MesloLGS NF without editing CSS. Stored in config.yaml as terminal.font_family. MOCR relevance: If we ever use the Hermes desktop app for MOCR, this saves the manual CSS config step.
  • #70924 — Timeline tick highlight fix (DavidMetcalfe) — Desktop: bottom-most tick in the timeline was missing its highlight when scrolled to end. Small polish fix.
  • #65906 — Hindsight: Browser "Connect" for desktop memory provider (benfrank241) — New connector for Hindsight's desktop memory provider. MOCR relevance: Hindsight integration could be interesting for persistent agent memory across MOCR sessions.

🛠️ Community Builds — This Week

  • design-judge-skills (⭐327) — Evidence-driven agent skills for design award workflows. Still the week's biggest debut by a wide margin.
  • frakio-work (⭐54) — Multi-agent workspace stitching Codex + Hermes + Arc. Gained a couple stars since morning.
  • hermes-improvements (⭐8) — Vector memory, adaptive soul, workflow classification for Hermes.
  • agent-hub-desktop (⭐4) — Codex/Hermes/Claude Code local desktop collaboration hub.
  • agent-console (⭐3) — Browser mission control: chat, sessions, cron, skills, voice for one Hermes agent.
  • hermespace (⭐3) — Pocket workbench: limited FOA desk, dual decode, skills+MEMORY fabric.
  • hermes-newton-operations (⭐2) — Profile-scoped operations dashboard and bounded decision journal for Hermes. MOCR relevance: A bounded decision journal is exactly the kind of pattern MOCR should consider.

📈 Gaining Popularity

  • hermex (⭐913) — Native iPhone app for Hermes. Now at 913 stars, +5 since morning. Steady climb.
  • EvoForge (⭐101) — Desktop agent workbench with evolutionary capabilities.
  • memmy-agent (⭐68) — Personal agent & local memory hub for Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, Hermes.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for coding agents: auto-capture, explainable recall, knowledge consolidation.
  • lyric-video-director (⭐43) — Hermes skill for typography-led lyric videos.
  • builder-essential-skills (⭐33) — Daily workflow skills for Hermes.

📚 Worth Reading

  • slashstack-skills (⭐3) — 11 MIT-licensed skills for coding agents: /preflight, /audit, /ship, /remember. Plain-markdown agent guardrails. Free, no dependencies, compatible with Claude Code too. /preflight and /audit patterns could map nicely to MOCR's own quality gates.

CrossWire: Tech — July 24 Evening

🍎 Apple

  • Siri settlement approved — The $250 million class action over delayed Siri AI launch received court approval. Eligible iPhone model owners can claim payouts. MacRumors has the eligibility details.
  • macOS 27 beta reworks Mail compose — Latest Golden Gate beta shows a redesigned formatting bar promoting Apple Intelligence Writing Tools and Siri AI directly in the compose window.
  • iOS 26.6 landing next week — RC seeded to developers. Release notes cite bug fixes, security updates, Spotlight optimization to prep for iOS 27, plus a new iMessage scam alert and an alert when blocking too many contacts.
  • A12/A13 unpatchable exploit lawsuit — Cybersecurity firm Paradigm Shift removed their blog post about the boot ROM vulnerability after being sued by digital forensics firm. Affects iPhone XR, XS, 11, and earlier iPad models — no fix possible in silicon.
  • watchOS 27 adds battery optimization — New feature coming this fall to improve Apple Watch battery life.

🤖 AI & Models

  • Claude Opus 5 announced — Anthropic released Opus 5 today, offering near-Fable-5 performance at half the cost (Fable 5: $10/$50 per MTok; Opus 5 pricing TBD but positioned well below). Built for daily use. This is the model that matters for anyone choosing between cost and capability on agentic workloads.
  • OpenAI hardware + Apple lawsuit — The MacRumors Show covers OpenAI's move into hardware and Apple's trade secret lawsuit, now reassigned to Judge Davila.

📡 Tech & Space

  • Most advanced robotic servicing satellite — Ars Technica profiles a satellite capable of refueling, repairing, and repositioning other spacecraft. "These are things that tend to be really hard."
  • Starship launch on deck — Rocket Report: Lightning strikes in China; Starship launch approaching; Relativity Space building a second rocket factory near Cape Canaveral.
  • Robot snakes searched Venezuela earthquake rubble — US robotics researchers flew to Venezuela with snakebots to search for survivors in collapsed buildings. Genuinely cool application of robotics.
  • AI data centers & reduced public input — Trump's EPA considering rule to let states decide how much public input is allowed on data center siting.
  • LG monitors installing McAfee via Windows Update — Microsoft investigating after monitor firmware pushed McAfee ads through Windows Update.

🏠 Home Automation

  • Home Assistant feed was unavailable this cycle (RSS parse error). No notable updates detected from other sources.

CrossWire: Commanders — July 24 Evening

🏈 Training Camp: 5 Days Out

Camp opens July 29 in Ashburn, VA. One week to go — the roster preview cycle is in full swing. Key dates: joint practice with Miami on Aug 12, joint practice with Baltimore at their facility on Aug 14.

Position Battles Heating Up

  • Edge rusher rotation — Dorance Armstrong returning from torn ACL, plus free-agent signees Odafe Oweh (Ravens) and K'Lavon Chaisson (Panthers), plus 5th-round pick Joshua Josephs. That's a lot of bodies competing for edge snaps in Daronte Jones' scheme.
  • Ground game emphasis — GMFB's burning question: can Jacory Croskey-Merritt and Rachaad White deliver on the offseason's explicit goal of a more involved running attack?

Team News

  • Laremy Tunsil ranked Top 5 tackle by ESPN — The blindside anchor getting national recognition.
  • Command Center Top 10 roster — Mitchell, Moss, and Smoot published their top 10 players on the current roster. The talent infusion this offseason is real.
  • John Riggins' #44 retirement — Week 9 (Nov 8 vs Rams) halftime ceremony. One of the most iconic players in franchise history.

2026 Schedule Snapshot

  • Opener: Sep 13 at Eagles
  • Home opener: Sep 27 vs Seahawks
  • Key divisional: Eagles twice (Weeks 1 & 9), Cowboys twice (Weeks 2 & 18), Giants twice (Weeks 6 & 10)
  • Riggins ceremony: Nov 8 vs Rams (Week 9)
  • Finale: Jan 10 vs Cowboys
  • 17 games remain, all scheduled.

Around the NFL

  • Patriots training camp intel — ESPN reporting live from Pats camp, tracking QB battles and potential breakouts.
  • Bills picking grass — GM Brandon Beane explained why Buffalo chose natural grass over turf for their new stadium.
  • Fantasy football season — ESPN's Liz Loza diving into Panthers, Texans, and Broncos backfield situations.
  • Caleb Williams hype — Can the Bears live up to expectations after last season's success?
  • Tim Hasselbeck on Seahawks — Making the case that Seattle is being overlooked.
